free_text: Systematic analysis of mass yield curves in low-energy
fission of actinides
Department of Chemistry, Faculty of Science
Gaussian width parameter 0.95 proposed in this
reference was used when correction was necessary for
charge distribution.
Reaction cross section data reported in this reference
were used to determine beam current
Decay data not given
Beam intensity is about 1 micro-Ampere.
- Target enrichment is larger than 99%.
- Chemical-form of target is element.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness is 0.100 to 0.300 mg/cm**2.
Thickness was determined by alpha spectrometry
- Backing is aluminium. Electrodeposited on Al foil
and wrapped with a sheet of Al foil
- Backing-thickness is 6 mg/cm**2.
Institute for Nuclear Study
Mass yield curves were constructed from the observed
cross section of each fission product to which a
correction was applied, when necessary, for the charge
distribution by assuming a Gaussian charge
distribution with the most probable charge Zp of
unchanged-charge division. The Gaussian width
parameter of 0.95 was used as suggested by McHugh et
al. [J.A. Mchugh and M.C. Michel, Phys. ReV.
To detect decay gamma
Weighted mean peak positions of heavy
asymmetric peak
See NRDF D1305 DATA,10 for detail.
Weighted mean peak positions of light
asymmetric peak
See NRDF D1305 DATA,11 for detail.

peak
See NRDF D1305 DATA,12 for detail.
